records.summary: SourcePlan is the neutral source artifact boundary; SourcePlanAssembly owns generated file assembly policy and configuration admission fragments before any host writes files.
records.rationale: App-builder, fixture materialization, diagnostics-to-action, and future editing all need source artifacts, but none of those callers should own ad-hoc file envelopes, text-authority propagation, or package-tooling side channels.
guidance: Start in source-plan when generated source needs file roles, edit policy, text authority, source-pattern metadata, or project tooling rather than app-builder-local file models.

Use SourcePlanAssembly when a generator creates multiple SourcePlanFile artifacts under one root, policy, and text-authority lifetime. This is the intended replacement for repeated SourcePlanFile/SourcePlanText constructor blocks in concrete source lowerers.

Use AppBuilderSourcePlanAssembly for app-builder generated-app or source-starting lowerers that need the app-builder baseline conflict/formatting/tooling policy, generated text authority, configured entrypoint admission, dependency evidence, and runnable project tooling. Concrete source lowerers should not restate that transaction locally.

Use SourcePlanContribution when generated file text is assembled from lower-level source facts. TypeScript imports and source fragments should stay available beside final text so MCP/IDE callers can explain which framework mechanism, app-builder part invocation, or app-builder source-lowering target produced a file without reparsing source.

Keep SourcePlan contributions and verification row families available internally, but do not assume every public SourcePlan answer should ship the full ledger. App-builder public SourcePlan projections expose generated text, contribution counts, witness counts, control-use counts, expected-effect counts/kinds, request-field summaries, and compact handoff/result-boundary notes by default; `includeSourcePlanContributions`, `includeSourcePlanWitnessRows`, `includeControlUseInventoryRows`, `includeExpectedEffectRows`, `includeSourceLoweringRequestFields`, and `includeDecisionBundleExpansionRows` are explicit provenance/verification detail flags.

Preserve both app-builder source-lowering invocation and app-builder source-lowering composition origins. A one-target control fragment and a top-level Native Submit Form wrapper are different provenance layers, and SourcePlanContribution should keep both visible beside delegated part-source origins.

Preserve direct app-builder source-lowering target origins when a fragment does not come from a part invocation or composition member. `SourceLoweringTarget` origins are the neutral handoff for generated support declarations such as local view-model state type aliases and option members.

Treat TypeScript top-level declaration fragments as a first-class generated-source category beside class-member fragments. Custom-element pair assembly should keep top-level declarations outside the class body while still carrying their SourcePlan contribution origins.

Use custom-element pair SourcePlan assembly when a source-lowering composition produces a template and companion invocations produce TypeScript class members. The pair assembler is a file/artifact boundary over existing fragments; it should not turn class-member invocations into standalone SourcePlan targets or hide the template/composition provenance layer.

Treat app-builder source-lowering `sourceLoweringTargetRefs` and `effectContractIds` as the read-model bridge into SourcePlan effects. They identify which ontology rows and promised effect contracts explain a generated fragment; they do not prove that the final SourcePlan has been reopened or verified.

Use TypeScriptImportRequirement plus typescript-import-source helpers for static import assembly. Source lowerers should publish import requirements and let source-plan merge them instead of formatting local import blocks. Preserve the distinction between value imports and type-only imports so generated app code does not create runtime imports for type surfaces.

The TypeScript import renderer groups package/bare imports before relative imports while preserving caller order within each group. Keep that grouping inside typeScriptImportStatements/mergeTypeScriptImportRequirements instead of making routed or app-builder source lowerers hand-sort imports.

Use TypeScriptSourceText/typeScriptSourceText when a complete generated TypeScript file is assembled from body text plus base imports and source fragments. That helper keeps import rendering and SourcePlanContribution rows in sync, including origin-bearing fragment imports, instead of forcing callers to collapse fragments into anonymous import requirements.

Use configuredAureliaEntrypointFile for entrypoint source. It spends the same TypeScriptImportRequirement lane for the default Aurelia import, root component import, configuration imports, and contribution-backed registration expressions.

Use aureliaRegistrationChain when entrypoint source needs to format Aurelia registration calls. A single simple registration such as RouterConfiguration should stay compact, while multiple registrations or multiline registration expressions should remain expanded.

Use the router-owned route-configuration source serializer when generated source needs `@route(...)` object literals. It owns multiline route-array/object formatting, including trailing commas, so app-builder routed fixtures do not grow local route text serializers.

Use SourcePlanProjectTooling for package dependencies, scripts, package manifests, browser root documents, build configs, tsconfig files, and local declaration files. Do not hide runnable-project requirements as ordinary app source files.

Use AureliaConfigurationAdmissionSource for framework/plugin entrypoint admission. App-builder source lowerers should request router, state, i18n, validation-html, or future configuration fragments instead of restating package imports, registration expressions, and dependency specifiers inline.

Preserve configuration admission source authority through SourcePlanContributionOriginKind.AureliaConfigurationAdmission. Configuration imports and registration expressions should carry AureliaConfigurationAdmissionKind origin rows so previews and future IDE/MCP affordances can explain entrypoint source without reparsing text.

Use SourcePattern on complete generated SourcePlans when callers need to distinguish source-text relation to caller domain, style, data, code economy, reusable modules, and analyzer pressure. SourcePattern should describe generated-source shape without owning app-builder menu taste.

Use SourcePatternParameter and sourcePatternParameterApplications when caller values are meant to adapt generated source. Source-applicable parameters must be reflected by the lowered SourcePattern source values, and non-applied/advisory/unknown/duplicate/invalid values should become typed workflow issues rather than silent fallbacks.

Use source-name.ts for generated name word-splitting and common case rendering before adding app-builder-local naming helpers. App-builder may expose convenience adapters, but the source-plan substrate owns the shared normalization policy because future edit planning and fixture materialization need the same source naming behavior.

When app-builder wraps app-builder source-lowering fragments in a SourcePlan preview, SourceRoot and SourceTargetPath may arrive either as resolved rootDir/templatePath transport fields or as SourcePlacement facet payloads. These are one placement model; conflicts should block with typed issues rather than establishing hidden precedence.

Keep SourcePlan neutral: app-builder owns generation intent and taste, fixture verification owns semantic promises, diagnostics-to-action owns repair intent, and future edit application owns host write policy.

SourcePlan contribution origins may carry raw string identities for product-specific concepts to preserve the neutral boundary. Product-specific coverage rows should split expected typed identities from observed raw origin text instead of widening product request or catalog types to anonymous strings.

A complete SourcePlan means the source text is available for preview/materialization; it is not permission to write files or overwrite user source. App-builder SourcePlan handoff notes may tell an AI/host to write returned `sourcePlan.files` rows and apply returned project tooling, but that instruction is a boundary statement for an external materialization surface, not an app-builder lowerer side effect.
App-builder SourcePlan contributions should carry current source-lowering target/invocation/composition origins, not deleted starter composition evidence.

Direct app-builder SourcePlan target admission is centralized through `directSourcePlanTargetFrame` plus descriptor-backed issue policy in `directSourcePlanTargetIssues`. New direct SourcePlan targets should add explicit descriptors for target support, missing/unknown/unsupported target issues, availability issues, and input-gate issues, then reuse the frame for preflight rows, target refs, and effect contract ids rather than cloning app-shell/router/state preflight checks.

`componentPairSourcePlan` is the app-builder component-pair phase that crosses into neutral SourcePlan artifact assembly. Earlier component-pair phases own selection, nested lowerings, local-state derivation, fragments, and issues; they should remain app-builder internals unless a future source/edit surface needs them directly.

Routed collection/detail SourcePlan expected effects should stay grouped by semantic promise family: app/state shape, route configuration source shape, route consumption/runtime reads, and template/runtime effects. These helpers describe post-reopen fixture-verification promises, not executable source-lowering policy.

anchors.summary: Neutral source artifact plan emitted before any host writes files.
Stateful source-plan assembler carrying policy and text authority across generated files.
App-builder source-plan transaction carrying generated-source policy, entrypoint admission, dependency evidence, and runnable project tooling.
File-local generated-source ledger for import requirements and source fragments before final text assembly.
Enum-backed contribution-origin families for app-builder part invocations, app-builder source-lowering invocations/compositions/direct targets, and framework configuration admissions.
App-builder generated-fragment origin vocabulary, including direct app-builder source-lowering target origins that map into SourcePlanContributionOriginKind.
App-builder generated-fragment kind vocabulary including TypeScript top-level declaration fragments.
App-builder source-lowering composition bridge whose fragments should preserve top-level composition and child invocation origins when assembled into SourcePlan files.
App-builder SourcePlan placement resolver that keeps direct transport fields and supplied SourcePlacement facets in one conflict-checked model.
App-builder SourcePlan selected-lowering frame that dispatches one explicit source-lowering branch before the neutral SourcePlan artifact is projected.
App-builder component-pair phase that assembles validated template/TypeScript fragments into neutral custom-element pair or root app-shell SourcePlan artifacts.
Shared direct SourcePlan target admission frame that owns preflight, preflight row, target refs, effect contract ids, and descriptor-backed target issues.
Shared direct SourcePlan target gate that maps target support, preflight availability, and input-gate states through explicit issue descriptors.
App-builder graph bridge from app-builder ontology target refs to associated effect contract ids for generated-fragment effect navigation.
Neutral static TypeScript import requirement merged by source-plan import assembly.
Generated TypeScript import renderer that groups package/bare imports before relative imports while preserving caller order within each group.
Neutral TypeScript source-text assembler that keeps rendered imports and source-plan contributions aligned.
Structured package/build sidecar for runnable generated projects, including package metadata, root documents, build config, tsconfig, and local declarations.
Neutral entrypoint imports, registrations, and package dependencies for framework/plugin admission.
Enum-backed framework/plugin admission identity carried by entrypoint source-plan contributions.
Entrypoint registration-chain formatter that keeps simple single registrations compact while preserving multiline registration expressions.
Router-owned route configuration object serializer for reviewable generated route trees.
SourcePlan-to-expected-effect bridge that reads routed collection/detail route parameter metadata before projecting fixture-verification promises.
Routed collection/detail route-configuration expected-effect group for router options, route config carriers, and viewport targets.
Routed collection/detail route-consumption expected-effect group for viewport declarations and route-parameter reads.
Source-plan origin constructor for framework configuration admission contributions.
Reusable source-pattern metadata carried by source plans without owning caller intent.
Proof row calculator for caller-supplied source-pattern values against lowered source.
Shared source-pattern module vocabulary for app shell, resources, router, state, forms, plugins, and composition.
App-builder custom-element SourcePlan pair assembler for companion TypeScript/template artifacts.
SourcePlan TypeScript class artifact builder that preserves imported top-level declaration and class-member fragment contributions.
Shared source-name word-splitting substrate for generated source plans.
Shared lower-camel renderer for generated source names.
Shared Pascal-case renderer for generated source names.
Shared kebab-case renderer for generated source names.
Focused contract proving framework/plugin configuration admissions keep SourcePlan contribution origins and compact simple registration formatting through generated entrypoints.
